entries.note: Memory includes working, episodic, semantic, and procedural forms.
Focus is selective; it always excludes something.
Models are useful because they omit detail.
Recall is usually harder than recognition.
Skill develops through feedback, repetition, and refinement.
Practice is most effective when it includes feedback.
Error correction is central to learning.
Feedback closes the loop between action and correction.

entries.clue: Applying learning in a new context
entries.note: Transfer is one sign that knowledge has become flexible.
